skill.name: performance-testing-review-multi-agent-review
skill.description: Multi-agent code review orchestration tool for comprehensive software artifact analysis
skill.summary: Coordinates specialized AI agents to perform holistic code reviews across security, architecture, performance, and quality dimensions
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 27 static analysis findings are false positives. The SKILL.md file is documentation containing Python pseudo-code examples in markdown blocks, not executable code. No actual command execution, cryptographic operations, or system reconnaissance patterns exist. Safe for publication.

content.user_title: Orchestrate multi-agent code reviews with AI
content.value_statement: Traditional code reviews miss critical issues by examining code from a single perspective. This tool coordinates multiple specialized AI agents to provide comprehensive analysis across security, architecture, performance, and quality dimensions.
actual_capabilities: Coordinates multiple specialized AI agents for parallel code analysis
Routes code to appropriate reviewers based on code characteristics
Consolidates findings from security, architecture, and performance agents
Resolves conflicting recommendations using weighted scoring
Supports file paths, Git repositories, and code snippets as input
limitations: Requires clear code context for accurate agent routing
Review quality depends on agent specialization coverage
Large codebases may require batch processing for complete analysis
Does not replace human judgment for final approval decisions

use_cases.title: Web Application Security Review
use_cases.description: Automatically route web application code through security auditors and web architecture specialists for comprehensive vulnerability assessment.

use_cases.title: Microservices Architecture Validation
use_cases.description: Validate microservices designs through sequential review phases covering design patterns, implementation quality, and deployment readiness.

use_cases.title: Performance-Critical Code Analysis
use_cases.description: Identify performance bottlenecks by routing performance-sensitive code through specialized performance analyst agents.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Code Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the code at [file path] using the multi-agent review system. Focus on identifying any critical issues and provide a summary of findings.

prompt_templates.title: Security-Focused Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a security audit on [repository URL] using the security-auditor agent. Identify potential vulnerabilities and provide remediation recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Full Architecture Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Conduct a comprehensive architecture review of [project path] using sequential agents: architect-reviewer for design patterns, code-quality-reviewer for implementation, and devops-validator for deployment considerations.

prompt_templates.title: Custom Agent Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure a custom review with parallel agents [security-auditor, performance-analyst] and sequential agent [architecture-reviewer] for [code snippet]. Weight security at 0.4, performance at 0.4, and architecture at 0.2.

output_examples.output: Security audit identified 2 critical issues: 1) Missing input validation on user credentials allowing injection attacks, 2) Weak password hashing using MD5. Recommended actions: Implement parameterized queries and upgrade to bcrypt.

Performance analysis found 3 optimization opportunities: 1) N+1 query pattern in user lookup, 2) Missing database indexes on frequently queried columns, 3) Inefficient loop processing large datasets. Estimated improvement: 60% reduction in query time.

best_practices: Define clear review objectives before selecting agent types
Use parallel execution for independent review dimensions to reduce turnaround time
Review consolidated reports and escalate conflicting recommendations for human judgment
anti_patterns: Running all agent types on every review without considering code characteristics
Ignoring agent confidence scores when prioritizing findings
Skipping sequential dependency phases when agent outputs build on each other

faq.question: What types of code can this tool review?
faq.answer: The tool accepts file paths, Git repository URLs, and direct code snippets. It automatically detects code characteristics to route to appropriate specialized agents.

faq.question: How does the multi-agent system resolve conflicting recommendations?
faq.answer: A conflict resolution engine applies weighted scoring based on agent expertise and context relevance. Complex conflicts are escalated for human review.

faq.question: Can I customize which agents participate in a review?
faq.answer: Yes, you can specify custom agent configurations with weights for each agent type based on your review priorities.

faq.question: How long does a typical review take?
faq.answer: Review time varies by codebase size and agent count. Parallel agents run concurrently, while sequential phases wait for previous outputs. Most reviews complete within minutes.

faq.question: Does this tool integrate with existing CI/CD pipelines?
faq.answer: The tool supports Git repository inputs and can be invoked from CI/CD workflows. Output reports can be formatted for integration with common code quality platforms.

faq.question: What happens if an agent times out or fails?
faq.answer: The system implements fallback mechanisms with intelligent timeouts. Failed agents are logged and the review continues with remaining agents, noting gaps in coverage.
